parts.text:
Role: You are acting as the Senior Android Lead for the "Mashashi" project.
​Instructions:
​Read and internalize the following project files: PROJECT.md, TECH_STACK.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, UX_GUIDELINES.md, and AGENTS.md.
​Initialize a new Kotlin-based Android project structure optimized for Jetpack Compose.
​Set the namespace to com.rohan.mashashi and minSdk to 26.
​Generate the build.gradle.kts (Project & App levels) including dependencies for:
​Jetpack Compose (Material 3)
​Hilt (Dependency Injection)
​Navigation Compose
​CameraX and ML Kit (Pose Detection)
​Create the MainActivity.kt and a ui/theme/ directory. Implement the "The Void" theme as defined in UX_GUIDELINES.md (True Black background, Inter/System font, and specific Hex codes).
​Scaffold the basic directory structure as defined in ARCHITECTURE.md (data, domain, ui).
​Output Requirement: Provide the terminal commands to create the directories and the full code for the Gradle files and the Initial Theme/MainActivity files.

Role: You are acting as the Senior Android Lead for the "Mashashi" project.
​Instructions:
​Read and internalize the following project files: PROJECT.md, TECH_STACK.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, UX_GUIDELINES.md, and AGENTS.md.
​Initialize a new Kotlin-based Android project structure optimized for Jetpack Compose.
​Set the namespace to com.rohan.mashashi and minSdk to 26.
​Generate the build.gradle.kts (Project & App levels) including dependencies for:
​Jetpack Compose (Material 3)
​Hilt (Dependency Injection)
​Navigation Compose
​CameraX and ML Kit (Pose Detection)
​Create the MainActivity.kt and a ui/theme/ directory. Implement the "The Void" theme as defined in UX_GUIDELINES.md (True Black background, Inter/System font, and specific Hex codes).
​Scaffold the basic directory structure as defined in ARCHITECTURE.md (data, domain, ui).
​Output Requirement: Provide the terminal commands to create the directories and the full code for the Gradle files and the Initial Theme/MainActivity files.

Role: Senior Android Systems Engineer.
​Context: Project "Mashashi" initialized. We are now implementing the "Warden" module for app monitoring and locking.
​Instructions:
​Create the Accessibility Service: Create data/services/WardenService.kt. This service must:
​Extend AccessibilityService.
​Monitor TYPE_WINDOW_STATE_CHANGED events to detect the package name of the foreground app.
​Log the package name to the console for now.
​Implement the Blackout Overlay: Create ui/components/BlackoutOverlay.kt. This should be a Compose-based View that:
​Covers the entire screen with #000000 (True Black).
​Displays the text "EVOLVE OR REMAIN THE SAME" in bold, white, modern typography.
​Has NO exit button.
​The Warden Controller: Create domain/manager/WardenController.kt to handle the logic:
​Define a whitelist containing com.whatsapp and the system dialer.
​Create a function shouldLock(packageName: String) that returns true if the app is NOT in the whitelist.
​Manifest Updates: Provide the XML code for AndroidManifest.xml to register the AccessibilityService and request the SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW (Draw over other apps) permission.
​Permissions UI: Update MainActivity.kt to include a simple setup screen that checks if the Accessibility and Overlay permissions are granted, with buttons to open the system settings for each.
​Output Requirement: Provide the complete code for the new files and the updated MainActivity.kt. Ensure the code follows the AGENTS.md standards (Senior level, clean logic).

input: Can you provide me the path to the project,also provide me the termux command for accessing the projects folder.

Check and inform me in which opperting system is kilo code cli running. You can use neofetch, install neofetch in termux amd use the tool to see the name of the Operating System and inform me the name.

parts.text:
Role: You are acting as the Senior Android Lead for the "Mashashi" project.
​Instructions:
​Read and internalize the following project files: PROJECT.md, TECH_STACK.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, UX_GUIDELINES.md, and AGENTS.md.
​Initialize a new Kotlin-based Android project structure optimized for Jetpack Compose.
​Set the namespace to com.rohan.mashashi and minSdk to 26.
​Generate the build.gradle.kts (Project & App levels) including dependencies for:
​Jetpack Compose (Material 3)
​Hilt (Dependency Injection)
​Navigation Compose
​CameraX and ML Kit (Pose Detection)
​Create the MainActivity.kt and a ui/theme/ directory. Implement the "The Void" theme as defined in UX_GUIDELINES.md (True Black background, Inter/System font, and specific Hex codes).
​Scaffold the basic directory structure as defined in ARCHITECTURE.md (data, domain, ui).
​Output Requirement: Provide the terminal commands to create the directories and the full code for the Gradle files and the Initial Theme/MainActivity files.

Role: Senior Android Systems Engineer.
​Context: Project "Mashashi" initialized. We are now implementing the "Warden" module for app monitoring and locking.
​Instructions:
​Create the Accessibility Service: Create data/services/WardenService.kt. This service must:
​Extend AccessibilityService.
​Monitor TYPE_WINDOW_STATE_CHANGED events to detect the package name of the foreground app.
​Log the package name to the console for now.
​Implement the Blackout Overlay: Create ui/components/BlackoutOverlay.kt. This should be a Compose-based View that:
​Covers the entire screen with #000000 (True Black).
​Displays the text "EVOLVE OR REMAIN THE SAME" in bold, white, modern typography.
​Has NO exit button.
​The Warden Controller: Create domain/manager/WardenController.kt to handle the logic:
​Define a whitelist containing com.whatsapp and the system dialer.
​Create a function shouldLock(packageName: String) that returns true if the app is NOT in the whitelist.
​Manifest Updates: Provide the XML code for AndroidManifest.xml to register the AccessibilityService and request the SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW (Draw over other apps) permission.
​Permissions UI: Update MainActivity.kt to include a simple setup screen that checks if the Accessibility and Overlay permissions are granted, with buttons to open the system settings for each.
​Output Requirement: Provide the complete code for the new files and the updated MainActivity.kt. Ensure the code follows the AGENTS.md standards (Senior level, clean logic).

Role: Senior Android Machine Learning Engineer.
​Context: Mashashi Project. We have the Warden monitoring apps. Now we need the "Physical Tax" mechanism using CameraX and ML Kit.
​Instructions:
​Create the Vision Engine: Create data/vision/PoseDetectorProcessor.kt. This class should:
​Initialize PoseDetector from Google ML Kit with AccuratePoseDetectorOptions.
​Process ImageProxy frames from CameraX.
​Extract coordinates for key joints (Shoulders, Elbows, Wrists, Hips).
​Implement Rep Logic: Create domain/logic/PushupCounter.kt. It must:
​Calculate the angle of the elbow joint using the Law of Cosines.
​Define a state machine: DOWN (angle < 90°) and UP (angle > 160°).
​Increment the count only when a full cycle (UP -> DOWN -> UP) is completed.
​Workout UI Component: Create ui/screens/WorkoutScreen.kt. It should:
​Integrate a CameraPreview using CameraX.
​Draw a glassmorphic overlay showing the current Rep Count and Target Count.
​Provide a "Posture Indicator" (e.g., a simple line that turns green when the joint angle is correct).
​Warden Integration: Update WardenController.kt so that if a "Blackout" is active, it can be dismissed only when PushupCounter reaches the target (e.g., 20 reps).
​Manifest: Add <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.camera" /> and the CAMERA permission to AndroidManifest.xml.
​Output Requirement: Provide the complete Kotlin code for the Vision Processor, the Logic class, and the Workout Screen. Ensure the math for joint angles is technically accurate (c^2 = a^2 + b^2 - 2ab \cos(C)).

Role: Senior Android Data Architect.
​Context: Mashashi Project. We have the Warden and the Vision Engine. Now we need to save progress and calculate Player Ranks.
​Instructions:
​Database Setup: Create data/local/MashashiDatabase.kt using Room. Define two tables (Entities):
​UserStats: To store level, totalXp, strengthXp, focusXp, wisdomXp, and disciplineXp.
​DailyLogs: To store daily completion of reps, focus minutes, and streaks.
​The Rank Engine: Create domain/logic/RankCalculator.kt. Implement the logic for Rank thresholds:
​Rank E: 0 - 500 XP
​Rank D: 501 - 1500 XP
​Rank C: 1501 - 5000 XP
​Rank B: 5001 - 15000 XP
​Rank A: 15001 - 50000 XP
​Rank S: 50001+ XP
​XP Logic: Create domain/usecase/AddExperience.kt.
​1 Pushup = 5 Strength XP.
​1 Minute Deep Work = 10 Focus XP.
​Completing a 90-day reset task = 100 Discipline XP.
​Dashboard UI: Create ui/screens/DashboardScreen.kt. Use the UX_GUIDELINES.md to design a high-end profile view:
​A large, glowing "Rank Letter" (e.g., S) using a custom Canvas glow effect.
​Progress bars for each of the four pillars (Strength, Focus, Wisdom, Discipline).
​A "Streak Counter" that visualizes the "90-Day Reset" progress.
​ViewModel Integration: Create ui/viewmodels/MainViewModel.kt to act as the single source of truth, connecting the Room DB to the UI.
​Output Requirement: Provide the code for the Entities, DAO, Database, Rank Logic, and the Dashboard Compose screen.

Role: Senior AI Integration Engineer.
​Context: Mashashi Project. We need to integrate the "AI Oracle" using the Gemini API to handle wisdom, reflections, and motivation.
​Instructions:
​AI Service Setup: Create data/remote/GeminiService.kt.
​Use the Google AI Client SDK.
​Implement a function generateGrowthFeedback(stats: UserStats, journalEntry: String) that returns a personalized, disciplined response.
​Journaling Module: Create ui/screens/JournalScreen.kt.
​A beautiful, minimalist text editor with glassmorphic cards.
​Features: Progress picture upload placeholder, "Analyze with AI" button.
​Navigation: Year-by-year navigation as defined in PROJECT.md.
​Wisdom Use Case: Create domain/usecase/ProcessReflection.kt.
​This should take a journal entry, send it to Gemini, and if the entry is meaningful (determined by AI), grant 25 Wisdom XP.
​Daily Affirmations: Create ui/components/DailyAffirmation.kt.
​A component on the Dashboard that fetches a "System Message" from Gemini every 24 hours based on the user's current Rank and Streaks.
​Security: Remind the user to place the API Key in a local.properties file or BuildConfig—never hardcode it.
​Output Requirement: Provide the Kotlin code for the Gemini Service, the Journal Screen, and the Wisdom Use Case. Ensure the UI matches "The Void" aesthetic.

Role: Senior DevOps Engineer.
​Context: Mashashi Project. We need to set up a GitHub Action workflow to compile our code into a testable APK.
​Instructions:
​Create the Workflow: Create a file at .github/workflows/android_build.yml.
​Workflow Logic:
​Trigger on push to the main or master branch.
​Use ubuntu-latest.
​Set up JDK 17.
​Cache Gradle dependencies to speed up builds.
​Run ./gradlew assembleDebug to generate a debug APK.
​Artifact Upload: Use actions/upload-artifact@v4 to make the app-debug.apk downloadable from the GitHub Actions tab.
​Git Initialization: Provide the terminal commands to initialize git, add the remote repository, and push the current code.
​Output Requirement: Provide the exact YAML content for the workflow and the terminal commands. Keep it concise to avoid context errors.

input: Task: Create the GitHub Actions build pipeline.Environment: Android / JDK 17 / Gradle.1. Create File:​
